channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Agentic AI Isn't Risky; the Way Orgs Deploy It Is
item.description: AI agents aren't black boxes — they're models interacting with software tools. The risk lies in their overlap....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver credential-stealing malware.
"The campaign ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
item.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How SIEM helps MSPs reduce noise and stop threats faster
item.description: MSPs don't lack security data. They struggle to separate real threats from alert noise. Kaseya explains how SIEM helps MSPs improve visibility, reduce fatigue, and respond faster. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
item.description: Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better underst...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
item.description: Every time you think the industry has finally stopped doing some reckless, low-effort crap, somebody spins up a fresh box full of sketchy loaders, fake installers, recycled social-engineering bait, an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Romanian gets 5 years in prison for hacking Oregon govt network
item.description: A Romanian national was sentenced this week to 56 months in federal prison for breaking into an Oregon state government computer network and fr cyberattacks targeting dozens of other U.S. victims. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
item.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: Why network incidents take too long to resolve
item.description: Many organizations can detect network issues quickly, but investigations and coordination often slow incident resolution. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT tea...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New AI Usage Report: Enterprise AI Risk Is Heavily Concentrated Among a Small Group of AI "Power users"
item.description: State of AI Usage Report 2026 (full report here) by LayerX Security reveals the extent of the enterprise AI visibility gap and why most organizations still don't understand where their AI exposure is ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Carnival Cruise confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
item.description: Carnival Corporation, the world's largest cruise line operator, has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang in April 2026. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Sextortionist sentenced to 33 years for targeting 145 children
item.description: A Canadian man was sentenced to 33 years in prison after pleading guilty to targeting more than 145 children across the United States, some as young as 6 years old, in an eight-year-long sextortion sc...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
item.description: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
item.description: A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social engineerin...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
item.description: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GPU mining malware spreads via SEO poisoning, AI chatbots
item.description: Threat actors are targeting systems with high-performance computers in an ongoing cryptojacking campaign spread through a coordinated SEO poisoning operation that also manipulated AI chatbot recommend...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
item.description: The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and social-engineering its way into servers and databases....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
item.description: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
item.description: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grandoreiro Malware and BTMOB RAT Campaigns Target Windows and Android Users
item.description:
Latin America and Europe become the target of two banking trojan campaigns that are designed to infect Windows and Android devices with Grandoreiro and BTMOB malware, respectively.
That's according t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious npm Package Stole Files From Claude AI User Directory via GitHub
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malicious package on the npm registry that comes with information stealing capabilities.
According to OX Security, the package, named "mouse5212-super-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
item.description: Strong Active Directory passwords don't have to come at the expense of usability. Specops Software explains how passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service resets can improve security ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee sho...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Glassworm botnet disrupted after resilient C2 infrastructure takedown
item.description: The Glassworm botnet targeting developers in software supply-chain attacks has been disrupted after researchers took down its resilient command-and-control infrastructure relying on Solana blockchain ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cybersecurity Evolution: How We Went From Perimeter Defense to AI-Native Security
item.description: The cybersecurity industry of 2006 barely resembled today's billion-dollar behemoth. As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary celebration, we trace the industry's evolution through a technology lens...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI warns of in-person data theft attacks from extortion gang
item.description: The FBI warned on Tuesday that the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) extortion gang is now targeting U.S.-based law firms in in-person data theft attacks. [...]...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown Disrupts Developer Supply Chain Attack Infrastructure
item.description: CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent soft...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 3 SOC Steps that Shut Down Incident Risks Early
item.description: Most organizations still picture cyber defense as a fortress problem: build stronger walls, add more guards, buy another detection engine. But modern incidents rarely crash through the front gate. The...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
